column1: # xlsx-imported cell comments paint a small corner marker (`'` in red)
# on the cell's right edge.  When the pointer lands on a commented cell,
# the comment text appears on control-panel line 3.
# exporter doesn't emit `xl/comments1.xml`, so we hand-inject it).
# Pointer lands on A1 (the cell with the first note).  The corner
# marker is suppressed on the highlighted cell (REVERSED stays loud),
# but the comment text appears on control-panel line 3.
# B2 (also has a comment) is *not* highlighted, so its corner marker
# C3 has no comment — no marker.
# Move pointer to B2 — comment text updates, A1's marker re-emerges.
# Move to C3 — no comment.  Both A1 and B2 markers appear (no
# pointer suppression on either).  We don't assert L3 cleared
# directly: the harness's substring assertion can't tell "blank" from
# "padded spaces".  The visible re-emergence of both markers is the
# proxy that proves the pointer is no longer on a commented cell.
